warning: Mathematical history can be reduced to a Greek-European sequence.
mitigation: Include Mesopotamian, Egyptian, Indian, Chinese, Islamic, African, Indigenous, and other traditions where relevant and well-sourced.

warning: Older mathematics can be judged only by modern notation, proof, or rigor standards.
mitigation: Interpret mathematical practices within their own media, genres, institutions, and purposes.

warning: Surviving written texts can be mistaken for the whole history of mathematical thinking.
mitigation: Attend to oral, material, craft, architectural, navigational, commercial, and pedagogical practices.

warning: Famous individuals and elite texts can obscure collective labor, commentary, teaching, translation, and institutions.
mitigation: Document transmission, commentary, pedagogy, tools, and communities of practice.

warning: Non-European mathematics can be appropriated, minimized, or forced into European categories.
mitigation: Preserve historical specificity and avoid treating European forms as the only standard of mathematical maturity.

warning: Formalization, computation, and proof assistants can be treated as the inevitable final form of mathematics.
mitigation: Recognize computation as a powerful medium that remains dependent on human choice, meaning, and responsibility.
